sampleEn: This page is promoted as a complete bilingual reference. It explains Ar Asset Resolution, ArResolver, resolver contexts, scoped caches, asset paths, resolved paths, URI/IRI resolvers, package-relative paths, and asset-loading debugging boundaries while retaining official API names and excerpts.
The Ar library is responsible for querying, reading, and writing asset data while hiding the physical storage mechanism from USD clients.

sampleEn: `EsfPropertyInterface` is a property abstraction for scene adapter implementations. It resembles the read-only `UsdProperty` interface, but it is not a USD authoring wrapper.
The public methods are called by the exec network compiler, making this class an adapter boundary rather than a general-purpose property utility.

sampleEn: This page is treated as a complete bilingual reference. Chinese explanations come first, while English API names, method names, type names, token literals, links, and selected official excerpts are retained for comparison with the OpenUSD Doxygen page.
`HdRenderBuffer` is a Hydra data-resource handle that render passes can render into. It is commonly used for AOVs, draw targets, depth/color/id buffers, or auxiliary renderer outputs.

sampleEn: This page is treated as a complete bilingual reference: Chinese explanations come first, while English API names, class names, method names, type names, token literals, links, and selected official English excerpts are retained for comparison with the OpenUSD Doxygen page.

`HdSceneDelegate` is the classic Hydra scene-data query adapter. It provides data exchange with the client scene graph; it is not a USD authoring layer, not the final renderer, and not the renderer backend.

`HdStDispatchBuffer` is an unsigned-integer VBO used by Storm for indirect dispatch data consumed by `MultiDrawIndirect` or `DispatchComputeIndirect`.

`HdStRenderPassState` is the Storm render-pass state object for rendering parameters shared among render passes. It is not scene data or a draw item; it is the state package bound around GPU / Hgi command execution.

`HdTask` represents a unit of work during a Hydra render. It is scheduled by `HdEngine::Execute()` and is distinct from USD prims, scene-delegate data objects, and schema wrappers.

`HdxPickFromRenderBufferTask` runs picking queries against pre-existing ID buffers. It reuses existing render-buffer data rather than redrawing the whole scene.

sampleEn: This page is promoted to a complete bilingual reference. It keeps official API names, data-member names, type names, links, and excerpts while adding Chinese-first coverage of Pcp composition-arc semantics.
`PcpArc` represents an arc connecting two nodes in the prim index. The arc is owned by a node, the source, and points to its parent node, the target, in the index.

sampleEn: This page is promoted from the full-site draft queue to a complete bilingual reference page. The Chinese layer covers the core role of `SdfLayer`, the official English description, major API groups, common reading pitfalls, and related type relationships while retaining English excerpts, API names, class names, method names, type names, token literals, and links for comparison with the official Doxygen page.

The official description defines `SdfLayer` as a scene description container. In this context, the container is not a runtime scene graph and not the composed view exposed by `UsdStage`; it is the lower-level layer file or anonymous layer that stores authored scene description. It can describe a component asset by itself, or combine with other layers through composition to form larger asset, sequence, or shot structures.

τ¼¼Σ╕אτºםσ«רµצ╣τפ¿Θאפµר»Σ╜£Σ╕║ `SdfLayer` Σ╕¡σ»╗σ¥אσעלΦ«┐Θק«σא╝τתה storage keyπאגσ║ץσ▒ג layer Σ┐¥σ¡רτתהµר» specs σעל fields∩╝לσ╛טσñתΦ»╗σזשµףםΣ╜£Θ£אΦªבσוטτפ¿ `SdfPath` µלחσ«תΓא£σף¬Σ╕¬ primπאבproperty µטצ targetΓא¥πאגσ¢áµ¡ñσנלΣ╕אΣ╕¬ path σ£¿Σ╕םσנל `SdfLayer` Σ╕¡σן»Φד╜σס╜Σ╕¡Σ╕םσנל authored opinions∩╝¢Φ╖»σ╛הσא╝τ¢╕σנלΣ╕םΣ╗úΦí¿σ║ץσ▒גµץ░µם«µ¥Ñµ║נτ¢╕σנלπאג
sampleEn: This page is promoted from the full-site queue to a complete bilingual reference page. The Chinese layer covers the official uses of `SdfPath`, path-value semantics, absolute and relative paths, prim/property/target/variant path syntax, path construction helpers, thread-safety notes, and its relationship to `SdfLayer`, `UsdPrim`, `UsdProperty`, and `Pcp` composition debugging while retaining English excerpts, API names, class names, method names, type names, token literals, and links.

The official introduction defines `SdfPath` as a path value used to locate objects in layers or scenegraphs. Path value means that it is copyable, comparable, and usable as a key; it does not own prim, property, or layer data, and it does not guarantee that the target object exists. Its role is to express object locations in the USD namespace using a uniform syntax.

sampleEn: This page now provides full bilingual coverage for `SdfPrimSpec` as an authored prim description in an `SdfLayer`, while preserving official API names, method names, type names, tokens, links, and necessary English excerpts.
`SdfPrimSpec` represents a prim description in an `SdfLayer`. It stores authored layer-level data and is not the composed `UsdPrim` view seen on a `UsdStage`.

`TfToken` Θאגσנט bounded number of strings used as fixed symbolsπאגΦ┐שΘחלτתה bounded σ╛טΘחםΦªב∩╝תtoken τפ¿Σ║מ schema tokenπאבattribute nameπאבmetadata keyπאבprimvar nameπאבrenderer option name Φ┐שτ▒╗σ¢║σ«תµטצσן»µמºΘ¢זσנט∩╝לΦאלΣ╕םµר»µקáΘשנσó₧Θץ┐τתהτפ¿µט╖Φ╛ףσוÑµצחµ£¼πאגµךךΣ╗╗µהןΣ╕┤µק╢σ¡קτ¼ªΣ╕▓Θד╜µ│¿σזלµטנ token∩╝לΣ╝תΦ«⌐ token table Θץ┐µ£ƒσó₧Θץ┐σ╣╢Θאáµטנσזוσ¡רσמכσך¢πאג
sampleEn: This page is promoted from the full-site queue to a complete bilingual reference page. The Chinese layer covers `TfToken` as a registered string handle, the boundaries of constant-time comparison, assignment, and hashing, the bounded fixed-symbol assumption, token table and memory pressure, the differences among `GetString()`, `GetText()`, and `data()`, the non-creating lookup semantics of `Find()`, container usage through `HashSet` / `Set`, empty-token conventions, and its foundational role in OpenUSD schema tokens, attribute names, metadata keys, and primvar names while retaining English excerpts, API names, method names, type names, token literals, and links.

The official description defines `TfToken` as a handle for a registered string. This handle is not just a thin wrapper around `std::string`; it is a lightweight object referring to a registered string entry. Its core value is that once a string enters the token registry, comparison, assignment, and hashing can operate on token identity instead of repeatedly processing characters for known symbols.

sampleEn: `TraceEventData` holds payload values that can be attached to `TraceEvent` records. It does not represent the event timing, thread, stack, or scope itself.
The constructor overloads create typed payload values. The selected type determines both `TraceEvent::DataType` and the correct accessor.

sampleEn: This page is promoted from the full-site queue to a complete bilingual reference page. The Chinese layer covers the official definition of `UsdGeomMesh`, point-based primitive semantics, points versus face-vertices, topology encoded by `faceVertexCounts` and `faceVertexIndices`, subdivision properties, creases/corners/holes, interpolation controls, schema-wrapper entry points, and relationships to `UsdPrim`, `UsdGeomPointBased`, and `UsdGeomPrimvarsAPI` while retaining English excerpts, API names, class names, method names, property names, token literals, and links.

The official description defines `UsdGeomMesh` as encoding a mesh with optional subdivision properties and features. This mesh is a typed schema wrapping a `UsdPrim` on a stage; it defines how mesh-related attributes are read and authored rather than owning geometry memory. `Define()` authors a typed prim on the stage, while `Get()` returns a `UsdGeomMesh` view for an existing prim.

sampleEn: This page now provides full bilingual coverage for `UsdGeomPrimvarsAPI` as the USD geometry API for creating, querying, and inheriting primvars, while preserving official API names, method names, tokens, type names, links, and necessary English excerpts.

`UsdGeomPrimvarsAPI` encodes geometric primitive variables as `UsdGeomPrimvar`. A primvar is a USD attribute with additional primvar semantics such as namespace, interpolation, element size, indices, and inheritance.

sampleEn: This page has been promoted to a complete bilingual reference. The Chinese coverage explains the official role of `UsdImagingDelegate` as the primary translation layer between Hydra (`Hd`) core and the USD scene graph, while preserving English API names and excerpts for direct comparison.

`UsdImagingDelegate` translates composed USD scene-graph data into information that Hydra `HdSceneDelegate` and `HdRenderIndex` can synchronize and query. It sits between USD composition results and Hydra rendering abstractions.

sampleEn: This page is promoted as complete bilingual reference coverage for UsdImagingNurbsPatchAdapter while retaining original API identifiers, type names, method names, tokens, code fragments, links, and necessary English source excerpts for comparison with the official Doxygen page.

The official summary says this class provides delegate support for UsdGeomNurbsPatch. In this bilingual edition, that means the class belongs to the USD Imaging adapter layer and translates authored UsdGeomNurbsPatch prim data into data consumable by UsdImagingDelegate, Hydra render-index paths, or scene-index data sources.

sampleEn: This page has been promoted to a complete bilingual reference. The Chinese coverage explains the official UsdPhysicsJoint class reference around joint schema responsibility, relationship targets, local joint frames, enabled/collision/break attributes, articulation participation, and the boundary between USD authoring and physics solver behavior while preserving English API names and excerpts for comparison.

`UsdPhysicsJoint` authors a physics schema prim that constrains rigid-body motion in USD scene description. The official phrase Γא£A joint constrains the movement of rigid bodiesΓא¥ describes the USD authoring schema, while actual constraint solving, stability, break events, and articulation initialization remain runtime responsibilities.

sampleEn: This page is promoted from the full-site queue to a complete bilingual reference page. The Chinese layer covers the official definition of `UsdPrim`, composed prim semantics, property/metadata/composition-arc access, lifetime and traversal, applied API schemas, instancing, and relationships to `SdfPrimSpec` and typed schema classes while retaining English excerpts, API names, class names, method names, type names, token literals, and links.

The official description calls `UsdPrim` the sole persistent scenegraph object on a `UsdStage`. In this context, persistent means that client code can hold a lightweight handle for accessing the currently composed prim on a stage. It is neither the raw spec inside a layer nor a copied container of every authored opinion; it is the runtime entry point to the composed result.

σו╕σ₧כτפ¿µ│ץµר»µךך `UsdStageCacheContext` τ╗סσ«תσט░µƒנΣ╕¬ cache∩╝לτה╢σנמΦ░דτפ¿ `UsdStage::Open()`πאגσ£¿Φ┐שΣ╕¬Σ╕ךΣ╕כµצחΣ╕¡∩╝לOpen µףםΣ╜£σן»Σ╗ÑσוטµƒÑµי╛σל╣Θוםτתה stage∩╝¢σªגµ₧£µ▓íµ£יσנטΘאגτ╗ףµ₧£∩╝לΣ╣ƒσן»Σ╗Ñµךךµצ░µיףσ╝אτתה stage σןסσ╕דσט░ cacheπאגΣ╣ƒσ░▒µר»Φ»┤∩╝לcache Σ╕םµ¢┐Σ╗ú `UsdStage::Open()`∩╝לΦאלµר»µפ╣σןרσ«דΣ╕מσו▒Σ║½ stage Θ¢זσנטΣ║ñΣ║עτתהµצ╣σ╝ןπאג
sampleEn: This page is promoted from the full-site queue to a complete bilingual reference page. The Chinese layer covers the role of `UsdStageCache` as a strongly concurrency safe `UsdStageRefPtr` collection, typical use with `UsdStageCacheContext` and `UsdStage::Open()`, stage lookup by root layer, session layer, `ArResolverContext`, and other matching criteria, the selection boundaries among `Find()`, `FindOneMatching()`, and `FindAllMatching()`, diagnostic usage of `Contains()`, `GetAllStages()`, and `GetId()`, the relationship between `Erase()`, `EraseAll()`, `Clear()`, and external reference lifetime, and the boundary between a stage cache, a layer cache, and a resolver cache while retaining English excerpts, API names, class names, method names, type names, and links.

The official text describes `UsdStageCache` as a strongly concurrency safe collection of `UsdStageRefPtr`. It caches references to already opened or published `UsdStage` objects, not `SdfLayer` objects, asset resolver results, or composition caches. This distinction prevents confusing stage reuse issues with layer loading or asset-resolution problems.

`UsdValidationError` is a structured entity returned by a validation task. It is associated with a `UsdValidationValidator`, but it is not the validator itself.

sampleEn: `VdfContext` is the parameter bundle passed to computation callbacks. It is the only API through which a computation function accesses its inputs.
`VdfNode` defines graph structure, while `VdfContext` is the runtime callback context used for value access, output production, and diagnostics.

sampleEn: `VdfGrapherOptions` configures the presentation emitted by `VdfGrapher`; it is not an evaluator and does not change graph execution or computed values.
The callback and node-limit types are filtering and styling hooks for generated graph output; they do not mutate the underlying `VdfNetwork` topology.

σו¼σו▒µƒÑΦ»óµמÑσןúτתהΘרוΦ»╗Θחםτג╣µר» node topologyπאג`GetInput()`πאב`GetOutput()`πאבΦ╛ףσוÑσטקΦí¿σעלΦ╛ףσח║σטקΦí¿σ╕«σך⌐Φ░דτפ¿µצ╣µטצΦ░דΦ»ץσ╖Ñσו╖µúאµƒÑΣ╕אΣ╕¬Φךגτג╣µת┤Θ£▓Σ║זσף¬Σ║¢ named ports∩╝¢Φ┐שΣ║¢σנםτº░σ╕╕τפ¿ `TfToken` Φí¿τñ║∩╝לσ║פΣ┐¥τץשσמƒµá╖πאגΦכÑσ¢╛Σ╕¡µƒנΣ╕¬Φ╛ףσח║µ▓íµ£יΦó½Φ«íτ«ק∩╝לΘªצσוטσ║פµá╕σ»╣τ½»σןúσנםτº░πאבΦ┐₧µמÑσ»╣Φ▒íπאבrequired input request σעל dependency mask∩╝לΦאלΣ╕םµר»Σ╗מ USD authoring σ▒גσ»╗µי╛σמƒσ¢áπאג
sampleEn: `VdfNode` is the abstract base class for nodes in a `VdfNetwork`. It describes graph topology and scheduling-facing node structure rather than a scene description prim, render prim, or schema object.

The phrase Γא£base class for all nodes in a VdfNetworkΓא¥ should be read as data-flow graph terminology. Inputs, outputs, dependency masks, network ownership, and node identity are the key concepts on this page.

sampleEn: `VdfReadWriteAccessor< T >` allows random access to output data. The output data belongs to Vdf evaluation/storage infrastructure, not to authored scene data.
The class is an accessor rather than an owning container. Ownership, scheduling, and semantic meaning are handled elsewhere in the Vdf graph.

sampleEn: This page has been promoted to a complete bilingual reference. The Chinese coverage explains `VtValueRef` as a non-owning, type-erased value view interoperating with `VtValue`, while preserving English API names and excerpts for direct comparison.

`VtValueRef` lets APIs accept values of arbitrary type without always constructing an owning `VtValue` or adding many overloads. It is a lightweight type-erased reference view, with lifetime responsibility left to the caller.

sampleEn: This local bilingual page explains the OpenUSD tutorial for authoring variants in Chinese first while retaining official API names, file names, prim paths, tokens, code, and links for comparison.
The tutorial continues from the property-authoring tutorial and uses the `HelloWorld.usda` layer plus `authorVariants.py` to author a variant set.

σ«רµצ╣Φ»┤µרמ `usdcat` µקóσן»Σ╗ÑµúאµƒÑ layer µצחΣ╗╢∩╝לΣ╣ƒσן»Σ╗ÑΘאתΦ┐ח `-o` σזשσח║µצ░µצחΣ╗╢πאגΦ╛ףσח║µצחΣ╗╢τתהµי⌐σ▒ץσנםσז│σ«תΣ║זσ╕╕ΦºהΦ╜¼µםóτ¢«µáח∩╝תµךך `.usda` σזשµטנ `.usdc` Σ╝תσ╛קσט░Σ║לΦ┐¢σט╢µצחΣ╗╢∩╝לµךך `.usdc` σזשµטנ `.usda` Σ╝תσ╛קσט░µצחµ£¼µצחΣ╗╢πאגΦ┐שΘחלτתהΦ╜¼µםóΣ╕םµר»µפ╣σןר scene µןןΦ┐░Φ»¡Σ╣י∩╝לΦאלµר»µךךσנלΣ╕אσÑק layer σזוσ«╣Θחםµצ░τ╝צτáבµטנσןªΣ╕אΣ╕¬σ¡רσג¿µá╝σ╝ןπאג
sampleEn: This page provides a Chinese-first reading path for the official Converting Between Layer Formats tutorial while retaining the USD file extensions, tool names, options, and sample filenames needed for comparison.

The important distinction is between filename extension, underlying file format, and layer content. A `.usd` file may contain either text or binary USD data, and USD detects the actual underlying format when opening it.

sampleEn: This page is a Chinese-first bilingual reproduction of the OpenUSD release tutorial End to End Example. English identifiers, commands, file names, script names, plugin names, code snippets, and links are retained for comparison.
The official tutorial uses a simplified pipeline to connect asset creation, inspection, scripted editing, set dressing, shot layering, and rendering.

ΘרוΦ»╗Φ┐שΘí╡µק╢∩╝לσוטµךך schema class τנזΦºúΣ╕║Γא£σ¢┤τ╗ץ UsdPrim τתהσ╝║τ▒╗σ₧כΦ«┐Θק«σ▒גΓא¥πאגUSD scenegraph Σ╕¡τ£ƒµ¡úσ¡רσ£¿τתהµר» primπאבattributeπאבrelationshipπאבmetadata σעל composition∩╝¢schema class σטשµךךΦ┐שΣ║¢σ║ץσ▒גµץ░µם«σלוΦúוµטנτ¿│σ«תτתה C++/Python API∩╝לΦ«⌐Φ░דτפ¿ΦאוΘאתΦ┐ח GetπאבCreateπאבApplyπאבHasAPI τ¡יµמÑσןúΦ«┐Θק«τ║ªσ«תσÑ╜τתהσ▒₧µאºσעלΦíלΣ╕║πאגσ«רµצ╣µןנσט░τתה UsdModelAPIπאבUsdGeomImageableπאבUsdGeomMesh τ¡י∩╝לΘד╜µר»ΘאתΦ┐חτפƒµטנσש¿µטצσנלτ▒╗µ£║σט╢σ╛קσט░τתה schema classπאג

sampleEn: This page provides a Chinese-first reading path for the official Generating New Schema Classes tutorial while retaining the original USD identifiers, commands, and file names needed for technical comparison.
A schema class is an API layer around a UsdPrim. It gives strongly named C++ and Python access to authored properties, relationships, metadata, and schema-defined fallbacks.

σ«רµצ╣σ╝אσñ┤σ╝║Φ░ד hard-coded typenames∩╝לµר»Σ╕║Σ║זΦ«⌐Φ»╗Φאוτ£כµ╕ו USD µצחΣ╗╢µצחµ£¼σעל Python authoring API Σ╣כΘק┤τתהτ¢┤µמÑσ»╣σ║פσו│τ│╗πאג`def Xform "hello"` Σ╕¡τתה `Xform` Σ╕מ `stage.DefinePrim('/hello', 'Xform')` τתהτ¼¼Σ║לΣ╕¬σןגµץ░σ»╣σ║פ∩╝¢`def Sphere "world"` Σ╕¡τתה `Sphere` Σ╕מ `stage.DefinePrim('/hello/world', 'Sphere')` τתהτ¼¼Σ║לΣ╕¬σןגµץ░σ»╣σ║פπאגΦ┐שΘחלτתה `Xform`πאב`Sphere`πאב`/hello`πאב`/hello/world` Θד╜µר»σן»σñםτמ░τתהµךאµ£»σ¡קΘ¥óΘחן∩╝לΣ╕םσ║פτ┐╗Φ»סµטצµפ╣σזשπאג
sampleEn: This local bilingual page explains the OpenUSD Hello World Redux tutorial in Chinese first while retaining official English identifiers, code, file names, paths, and links for comparison.
The tutorial revisits Hello World and uses hard-coded type names, `Xform` and `Sphere`, that match the type names following `def` in the `usda` representation.

The tutorial walks through creating a simple USD stage containing a transform and a sphere.

sampleZh: µ£¼Θí╡µךךσ«רµצ╣ Houdini USD Example Workflow µץשτ¿כµץ┤τנזµטנΣ╕¡µצחΣ╕╗ΘרוΦ»╗Φ╖»σ╛ה∩╝לσ╣╢Σ┐¥τץשσ┐וΦªבΦכ▒µצחσמƒµצחπאבσס╜Σ╗ñπאבΦ╖»σ╛הσעלΣ║ºσףבσנםπאגσ«דΣ╕םµר»τמ░Σ╗ú Houdini Θ¢זµטנτתהµ£אµצ░µמ¿Φםנµ╡בτ¿כ∩╝לΦאלµר»Σ╕אΣ╕¬σמזσן▓µץשτ¿כ∩╝לτפ¿µ¥ÑΦ»┤µרמ USD Σ╕¡σ┐דτ«íτ║┐µאמµá╖Φ«⌐ DCC σ╖Ñσו╖σן¬σזשσוÑσ▒אΘד¿Φªזτ¢צσ▒ג∩╝לσזםµךךΦªזτ¢צσ▒גσנטσוÑ shot τתהτ╗הσנטτ╗ףµ₧£πאג

µץשτ¿כτתהσוÑσןúµר»τñ║Σ╛כ shot∩╝לΦאלΣ╕םµר»Σ╕אΣ╕¬σ¡ñτ½כτתה Houdini µצחΣ╗╢πאגσס╜Σ╗ñ usdview USD/extras/usd/tutorials/Houdini/shot.usda τפ¿µ¥Ñµיףσ╝אσמƒσºכ USD shotπאגΦ»╗Φאוσ║פΦ»Ñσוטσ£¿ usdview Σ╕¡Φºגσ»ƒσ£║µש»τ╗ףµ₧ה∩╝לσזםΦ┐¢σוÑ HoudiniπאגΦ┐שµá╖σבתτתהτ¢«τתה∩╝לµר»Φ«⌐τ╝צΦ╛סΦאוσוטτí«Φ«ñ prim pathπאבlayer composition σעלσן»ΦºבτתהΦ╡הΣ║ºσו│τ│╗∩╝לΘב┐σוםσ£¿ DCC Σ╕¡σח¡ΦºזΦºיτל£µ╡כσמ╗τ╝צΦ╛סΘפשΦ»»σ»╣Φ▒íπאג
sampleEn: This page provides a Chinese-first reading path for the official Houdini USD Example Workflow while retaining the original English terms, command, paths, and product names needed for comparison.

The first reading boundary is historical. The official page states that this tutorial is not regularly tested, and that the Houdini USD plugin was removed from USD in version 20.05 in favor of Houdini Solaris native USD support.

µ£¼µץשτ¿כµמÑσ£¿ `Hello World - Creating Your First USD Stage` Σ╣כσנמπאגΣ╕ךΣ╕אΘí╡σן¬Φ»בµרמΣ║זσªגΣ╜ץτפ¿ Python σט¢σ╗║Σ╕אΣ╕¬σ╛טσ░ןτתה stage∩╝¢µ£¼Θí╡σ╝אσºכµךךΦ┐שΣ╕¬ stage σ╜ףΣ╜£σ╖▓σ¡רσ£¿τתה USD µץ░µם«µ¥ÑµúאµƒÑσעלΣ┐«µפ╣πאגΦ╡╖τג╣µצחΣ╗╢µר» `extras/usd/tutorials/authoringProperties/HelloWorld.usda`∩╝לΘוםσÑקΦהתµ£¼µר» `extras/usd/tutorials/authoringProperties/authorProperties.py`πאגΘרוΦ»╗µק╢Φªבµךךσ«דτ£כΣ╜£Γא£Σ╗מσ╖▓Σ┐¥σ¡ר layer Θחםµצ░µיףσ╝אπאבΦ»╗σןצπאבσט¢Σ╜£πאבσזםΣ┐¥σ¡רΓא¥τתהΘק¡τמ»∩╝לΦאלΣ╕םµר»Θחםµצ░σט¢σ╗║ stage τתהµץשτ¿כπאג

Φ┐שΘí╡ΦªבΦ»╗µטנΓא£usdview µי⌐σ▒ץτג╣µץשτ¿כΓא¥∩╝לΣ╕םµר»Σ╕אΦט¼ Python Φהתµ£¼µץשτ¿כπאגusdview µןעΣ╗╢Φ┐נΦíלσ£¿ usdview Φ┐¢τ¿כΘחל∩╝לΦד╜Φ«┐Θק«σ╜ףσים stageπאבΘאיµכ⌐τך╢µאבπאבΦºזσ¢╛Φ«╛τ╜«σעל Qt Σ╕╗τ¬קσןú∩╝לσ¢áµ¡ñσ«דΘאגσנטσבתµúאµƒÑπאבΦ╛וσך⌐σן»Φºזσלצπאבµט¬σ¢╛πאבµי╣Θחןτך╢µאבΦ╛ףσח║µטצσזוΘד¿σ╖Ñσו╖σוÑσןúπאגσ«דΣ╕םσ║פΦ»ÑΦó½Φ»»ΦºúΣ╕║Σ┐«µפ╣ USD µá╕σ┐דµá╝σ╝ןτתהµ£║σט╢∩╝¢µןעΣ╗╢σן¬µר»τ╗ש usdview σךáσס╜Σ╗ñσעל UI∩╝לσ╣╢ΘאתΦ┐ח usdviewApi Σ╕מσ╜ףσיםΣ╝תΦ»¥Σ║ñΣ║עπאג

`Collections and Patterns` Φºúσז│τתהµר»Γא£σªגΣ╜ץσ£¿ USD σ£║µש»ΘחלΦí¿Φ╛╛Σ╕אτ╗הσ»╣Φ▒íΓא¥τתהΘק«ΘóרπאגΦ┐שΘחלτתהΣ╕אτ╗הσ»╣Φ▒íσן»Σ╗Ñµר» prim∩╝לΣ╣ƒσן»Σ╗Ñµר» property∩╝לΦ┐רσן»Σ╗ÑΘאתΦ┐חσלוσנ½σו╢Σ╗צ collection µ¥Ñτ╗הσנטµ¢┤σñºτתהΘא╗Φ╛סΘ¢זσנטπאגσ«דΣ╕םµר»σםץτ║»τתהτ¢«σ╜ץσטזτ╗הσךƒΦד╜∩╝לΦאלµר»Σ╕אτºםΘ¥óσנסΣ╕כµ╕╕σ╖ÑΣ╜£µ╡בτתהΘ¢זσנטΦí¿Φ╛╛µ£║σט╢∩╝לσ╕╕Φºבτפ¿Θאפσלוµכ¼ light-linkingπאבmaterial bindingπאבµ╕▓µƒףΘאיµכ⌐πאבµי╣ΘחןµúאµƒÑσעלσ╖Ñσו╖Σ╛ºτתהΘא╗Φ╛סΦ┐חµ╗ñπאג

σ«רµצ╣Θí╡Θªצσוטσ╝║Φ░ד collection τתהτב╡µ┤╗µאº∩╝תΣ╕אΣ╕¬µ¿íσ₧כτתהΓא£Θא╗Φ╛סΘד¿Σ╗╢Γא¥Σ╕םΣ╕אσ«תµ▓┐ scene hierarchy Φ┐₧τ╗¡σטזσ╕ד∩╝לµƒנΣ╕¬τב»σויσ╜▒σףםτתהσחáΣ╜ץΣ╜ףΣ╣ƒσן»Φד╜µ¥ÑΦח¬σñתΣ╕¬ layer µטצσñתΣ╕¬σס╜σנםτ⌐║Θק┤σטזµפ»πאגσªגµ₧£σן¬Θ¥áτט╢σ¡נσ▒גτ║º∩╝לσ╛טΘת╛Φí¿Φ╛╛Φ┐שΣ║¢Φ╖¿σ▒גτ║ºπאבΦ╖¿τ▒╗σ₧כπאבΦ╖¿Φ╡הΣ║ºτתהΘ¢זσנטπאגσ¢áµ¡ñ `CollectionAPI` µןנΣ╛¢Σ║זΣ╕אΣ╕¬τכ¼τ½כΣ║מτי⌐τנזσ▒גτ║ºτתה membership µןןΦ┐░σ▒ג∩╝לΦ«⌐σ╖Ñσו╖σן»Σ╗Ñτפ¿σנלΣ╕אσÑקΦºהσטשΦ»óΘק«Γא£σף¬Σ║¢σ»╣Φ▒íσ▒₧Σ║מΦ┐שΣ╕¬Θ¢זσנטΓא¥πאג

sampleEn: `CylinderLight` is an intrinsic light that emits outward from the side of a cylinder whose major axis is the local X axis.
Use it for tube-shaped, line-like, panel-like, or commercial interior light sources that have cylinder-side emission.

τñ║Σ╛כσ▒גΣ╕¡µ£יΣ╕אΣ╕¬ `def DiskLight "Light1"`∩╝לΦ«╛τ╜« `float inputs:radius = 0.8`πאב`color3f inputs:color = (1, 1, 1)`πאב`float inputs:intensity = 20.0`∩╝לσזםΘאתΦ┐ח `double3 xformOp:translate = (4, 0, 1)` µךךτב»µפ╛σט░ `Sphere` σעל `Cube` ΘשהΦ┐סπאגΦ┐שµ«╡τñ║Σ╛כΦ»┤µרמΣ║זΦבלΦ┤úΦ╛╣τץל∩╝ת`inputs:radius` Φ┤ƒΦ┤úσ£זτ¢רσñºσ░ן∩╝ל`inputs:intensity` σעל `inputs:color` Φ┤ƒΦ┤úΦד╜ΘחןσעלΘó£Φי▓∩╝ל`xformOp:translate` Φ┤ƒΦ┤úΣ╜םτ╜«∩╝ל`xformOpOrder` Φ┤ƒΦ┤úσןרµםóΘí║σ║ןπאגΣ╕¡µצחΘí╡ΦºúΘחךΦ┐שΣ║¢σ¡קµ«╡µק╢σ┐וΘí╗Σ┐¥τץש API/token/σ▒₧µאºσנםσמƒµá╖∩╝לΣ╕םΦד╜µךךσן»σñםσט╢τתה USD Φ»¡µ│ץτ┐╗Φ»סµטנΣ╕¡µצחµáחΦ»זτ¼ªπאג
sampleEn: `DiskLight` is an intrinsic light that emits from one side of a circular disk in the local XY plane.
Typical uses include soft-box-like, panel-like, and circular area-light setups.

σ«רµצ╣τפ¿Θאפµ«╡µךך `DistantLight` µפ╛σ£¿ sunlight σעל distant, broad sources of light τתהΦ»¡σóדΘחל∩╝תσויτ║┐σ╜▒σףםµץ┤Σ╕¬ scene∩╝לσ╣╢Σ╕פσñºΦח┤ parallel∩╝לσ╣│Φíלτ¿כσ║ªσןצσז│Σ║מ `inputs:angle`πאגΦ┐שΘחלτתהσו│Θפ«Φ╛╣τץלµר»Γא£Φ┐£σñהµצ╣σנסσויΓא¥Σ╕מΓא£σ▒אΘד¿Θ¥óτº»σויΓא¥τתהσל║σט½πאג`RectLight`πאב`DiskLight`πאב`SphereLight` σעל `CylinderLight` Θד╜µ£יσו╖Σ╜ףσ▒אΘד¿σחáΣ╜ץ∩╝¢`DistantLight` τתהΘרוΦ»╗Θחםτג╣σטשµר»µצ╣σנסπאבΦºעτ¢┤σ╛הπאבσ╝║σ║ªσעלΘר┤σ╜▒Φ╜»σלצπאגσ«דΘאגσנטσבתσñ¬Θר│πאבµ£טσויµטצσו╢Σ╗צµ₧בΦ┐£σויµ║נτתהΣ╕╗µצ╣σנסτוºµרמ∩╝לΣ╕םΘאגσנטΦí¿Φ╛╛Σ╕אΣ╕¬Θ¥áΦ┐סτי⌐Σ╜ףπאבΘתןΦ╖¥τª╗µרמµר╛Φí░σחןτתהτב»σו╖πאג

σ«רµצ╣τñ║Σ╛כσזשΣ║ז `def DistantLight "Light1"`∩╝לµךך `float inputs:angle = 1.0`∩╝לσזםµךך `float inputs:intensity = 10000`πאגΦ»┤µרמµצחσ¡קµלחσח║∩╝תτñ║Σ╛כµךך `inputs:angle` µןנΘ½רσט░ `1.0`∩╝לΦ┐שΣ╝תµרמµר╛µכףσ«╜ light spread σ╣╢Φ╜»σלצ shadow∩╝¢σנלµק╢σ«דµךךσ╝║σ║ªΣ╗מΘ╗רΦ«ñ `50000` Θשםσט░ `10000`πאגΦ┐שΣ╕םµר»ΘתןµהןΦ░דσןג∩╝לΦאלµר»σ£¿σ▒ץτñ║ `inputs:angle` σעל `inputs:intensity` τתהΦבפσך¿∩╝תΦºעσ║ªσןרσñºµק╢σויτ║┐Σ╕םσזםΘגúΣ╣טσ╣│Φíל∩╝לΘר┤σ╜▒µ¢┤Φ╜»∩╝לσ£║µש»σויΘחןΣ╣ƒσן»Φד╜σó₧σךá∩╝לσ¢áµ¡ñΘ£אΦªבΘשםΣ╜מ intensity µטצ exposure µ¥ÑΦíÑσב┐πאג
sampleEn: `DistantLight` provides light from a distant source along the local `-Z axis`, and is also known as a directional light.
It is typically used for broad distant sources such as sunlight, where rays affect the entire scene and are roughly parallel.

`LightAPI` µר»τ╗ש prim σó₧σךáΓא£τב»σויΦד╜σך¢Γא¥τתה API schemaπאגσ║פτפ¿σנמ∩╝לΦ»Ñ prim Σ╕םσן¬µר»σñתΣ║זσחáΣ╕¬µש«Θאתσ▒₧µאº∩╝לΦאלµר»Φó½ΦºזΣ╕║Φד╜σñƒσןגΣ╕מ usdLux σויτוºΦ»¡Σ╣יτתהσ»╣Φ▒í∩╝תσ«דσן»Σ╗Ñµ£י `inputs:color`πאב`inputs:intensity`πאב`inputs:exposure`πאב`inputs:diffuse`πאב`inputs:specular` τ¡יΦ╛ףσוÑ∩╝לΣ╣ƒσן»Σ╗ÑΘאתΦ┐ח `collection:lightLink`πאב`collection:shadowLink`πאב`light:filters` τ¡יσו│τ│╗Σ╕מσ£║µש»σחáΣ╜ץπאבΘר┤σ╜▒σעל filter τ╜סτ╗£σןסτפƒΦבפτ│╗πאג

σ«רµצ╣Θí╡τתהτ¼¼Σ╕אσ▒גµהןµא¥µר»∩╝ת`LightAPI` µך╜σןצΣ║זσñתτºםτב»σוי schema τתהσו▒σנלΦד╜σך¢πאגµקáΦ«║σו╖Σ╜ףτב»σויµר» `DiskLight`πאב`RectLight`πאב`SphereLight`πאב`DistantLight`πאב`DomeLight`∩╝לΦ┐רµר» geometry light τ¢╕σו│τתה API∩╝לσ«דΣ╗¼Θד╜Θ£אΦªבµƒנΣ║¢Θאתτפ¿Φ╛ףσוÑµ¥ÑµןןΦ┐░Θó£Φי▓πאבΣ║«σ║ªπאבµ¢¥σויσעלσן»Φºבσ╜▒σףםΦלדσ¢┤πאגτנזΦºú `LightAPI` σנמ∩╝לσזםΦ»╗σו╖Σ╜ףτב»σויΘí╡Θ¥óµק╢σ░▒Σ╕םΣ╝תµךךµ»ןΣ╕¬σ▒₧µאºσ╜ףµטנσ¡ñτ½כσ¡קµ«╡∩╝לΦאלΦד╜τ£כσח║σ«דΣ╗¼σ£¿τ╗ƒΣ╕אσויτוºµ¿íσ₧כΣ╕¡τתהΦºעΦי▓πאג

When `LightAPI` is applied to a prim, that prim gains light capabilities such as color, intensity, specular and diffuse multipliers, light-linking, and light filters.

sampleZh: µ£¼Θí╡µר» OpenUSD release τפ¿µט╖µלחσםקΣ╕¡ usdLux `PluginLightFilter` τתהσ«לµץ┤σןלΦ»¡σןגΦאדΘí╡πאגΣ╕¡µצחΣ╝רσוטΦºúΘחךσ«דσªגΣ╜ץµךך `LightFilter` τתהΘאתτפ¿Φ┐חµ╗ñΦ»¡Σ╣יΦ┐₧µמÑσט░σñצΘד¿ `SdrShadingNode` definition∩╝לσ╣╢ΘאתΦ┐ח `UsdShadeNodeDefAPI` Φ«⌐ render delegates Φ»זσט½µןעΣ╗╢σ«₧τמ░∩╝¢σנלµק╢Σ┐¥τץשσ«רµצ╣µáחΘóרπאבsectionπאבAPI/schema/token/σ▒₧µאºσנםπאבΣ╗úτáבσעלΘף╛µמÑσמƒµá╖πאג

`PluginLightFilter` τתהµá╕σ┐דΦבלΦ┤úΣ╕םµר»Θחםµצ░σ«תΣ╣יΣ╕אτºםσ¢║σ«תτתהτב»σויΦ┐חµ╗ñτ«קµ│ץ∩╝לΦאלµר»τ╗שµןעΣ╗╢σ₧כ light filter µןנΣ╛¢Σ╕אΣ╕¬σן»σןסτמ░πאבσן»σú░µרמπאבσן»Σ║ñτ╗שµ╕▓µƒףτ½»ΦºúΘחךτתה schema σוÑσןúπאגσ«רµצ╣τ¼¼Σ╕אµ«╡Φ»┤σ«דµןנΣ╛¢Σ╕אΣ║¢σ▒₧µאº∩╝לΣ╜┐ prim Φד╜σñƒΘאתΦ┐ח `UsdShadeNodeDefAPI` µáחΦ»זΣ╕אΣ╕¬σñצΘד¿ `SdrShadingNode` definition∩╝לσ╣╢µךךΦ┐שΣ╕¬ definition µןנΣ╛¢τ╗ש render delegatesπאגµםóσןÑΦ»¥Φ»┤∩╝לOpenUSD σ£¿Φ┐שΘחלΦí¿Φ╛╛τתהµר»Γא£Φ┐שΣ╕¬ filter τתהσ«₧τמ░τפ▒σñצΘד¿ Sdr Φךגτג╣σ«תΣ╣יΓא¥∩╝לΦאלΣ╕םµר»µךךµ»ןΣ╕אτºם renderer-specific filter Θד╜σ¢║σלצµטנτכ¼τ½כ schemaπאג

ΘרוΦ»╗µ£¼Θí╡µק╢σ║פσוטµךך `PluginLightFilter` σעלΣ╕ךΣ╕אΘí╡ `LightFilter` σטזσ▒גτנזΦºú∩╝ת`LightFilter` Φ┤ƒΦ┤úΘאתτפ¿Φ┐חµ╗ñσש¿Φ»¡Σ╣י∩╝לσלוµכ¼ `filterLink` collectionπאב`lightFilter:shaderId`πאבΣ╕מτב»σויτתה `light:filters` σו│τ│╗Σ╗ÑσןךΣ╗מ `Xformable` / `Imageable` τ╗ºµי┐τתהΘאתτפ¿ scene graph σ▒₧µאº∩╝¢`PluginLightFilter` σטשΦ┐¢Σ╕אµ¡ÑΦ»┤µרמΓא£σו╖Σ╜ףσ«₧τמ░σªגΣ╜ץΘאתΦ┐חσñצΘד¿Φךגτג╣σ«תΣ╣יµת┤Θ£▓τ╗ש rendererΓא¥πאגσ¢áµ¡ñσ«דµר»µי⌐σ▒ץσ▒ג∩╝לΣ╕םµר»σƒ║τíאΦ┐חµ╗ñΦ»¡Σ╣יτתהµ¢┐Σ╗úσףבπאג
sampleEn: This local bilingual page explains the usdLux `PluginLightFilter` guide in Chinese first while retaining official identifiers, section names, property names, tokens, and links for comparison.

`PluginLightFilter` identifies an external `SdrShadingNode` definition through `UsdShadeNodeDefAPI` so render delegates can consume a light-filter implementation without a dedicated schema for every filter type.

sampleZh: µ£¼Θí╡µר» OpenUSD release τפ¿µט╖µלחσםקΣ╕¡ usdLux `PortalLight` τתהσ«לµץ┤σןלΦ»¡σןגΦאדΘí╡πאגΣ╕¡µצחΣ╝רσוטΦºúΘחך `PortalLight` Σ╜£Σ╕║ local XY plane Σ╕¡τƒ⌐σ╜ó portal τתהτפ¿Θאפπאבσ«דσªגΣ╜ץσ╝ץσ»╝ `DomeLight` Θחחµá╖πאבΣ╕║Σ╜ץσנס local `-Z` direction Σ╝áσוי∩╝לΣ╗Ñσןך `inputs:height`πאב`inputs:width`πאב`light:shaderId` σעלτ╗ºµי┐σ▒₧µאºτתהΘרוΦ»╗Φ╛╣τץל∩╝¢σנלµק╢Σ┐¥τץשσ«רµצ╣µáחΘóרπאבsectionπאבAPI/schema/token/σ▒₧µאºσנםπאבΣ╗úτáבσעלΘף╛µמÑσמƒµá╖πאג

`PortalLight` τתהµá╕σ┐דΦבלΦ┤úµר»τ╗ש `DomeLight` µןנΣ╛¢Θחחµá╖σ╝ץσ»╝πאגσ«רµצ╣Θí╡µךךσ«דσ«תΣ╣יΣ╕║Σ╜םΣ║מ local XY plane τתהτƒ⌐σ╜ó portal∩╝לτפ¿µ¥Ñ guide sampling of a dome light∩╝לσ╣╢Φ»┤µרמσ«דσנס local `-Z` direction Σ╝áΘאעσויπאגΦ┐שΘחלτתהΓא£Σ╝áΘאעΓא¥Σ╕םσ║פτנזΦºúµטנµש«ΘאתΘ¥óτº»τב»τ¢┤µמÑσןסσוי∩╝לΦאלµר»σסךΦ»יµ╕▓µƒףσש¿∩╝תτמ»σóדσויΘאתΦ┐חΦ┐שΣ╕¬Θק¿πאבτ¬קπאבσ╝אσןúµטצΘחחµá╖τ¬קσןúΦ┐¢σוÑσ£║µש»µק╢∩╝לσ║פµ¢┤Θ¢זΣ╕¡σ£░Σ╗מΦ»Ñτƒ⌐σ╜óσל║σƒƒΘחחµá╖πאג

σ¢áµ¡ñ∩╝ל`PortalLight` σ┐וΘí╗σעל `DomeLight` Σ╕אΦ╡╖τנזΦºúπאג`DomeLight` µןןΦ┐░τמ»σóדσויµ║נ∩╝ל`PortalLight` µןןΦ┐░τמ»σóדσויΘחחµá╖Φ┐¢σוÑσ£║µש»τתהτ⌐║Θק┤τ¬קσןú∩╝¢Σ╕ñΦאוτ╗הσנטσנמ∩╝לµ╕▓µƒףσש¿σן»Σ╗Ñσ£¿σ«ñσזוτ¬קµט╖πאבΘק¿µ┤₧µטצσ╝אσןúΘשהΦ┐סµ¢┤Θ½רµץטσ£░Θחחµá╖τמ»σóדσויπאגσםץτכ¼µפ╛τ╜«Σ╕אΣ╕¬ `PortalLight` σ╣╢Σ╕םτ¡יΣ╗╖Σ║מσט¢σ╗║Σ╕אΣ╕¬µצ░τתהµש«ΘאתσןסσויΘ¥ó∩╝¢σ«דΘ£אΦªבΦó½µ╢טΦ┤╣τ½»µלי dome-light portal Φ»¡Σ╣יΦºúΘחךπאג
sampleEn: This local bilingual page explains the usdLux `PortalLight` guide in Chinese first while retaining official identifiers, section names, property names, tokens, and links for comparison.
A `PortalLight` is a rectangular portal in the local XY plane that guides sampling of a dome light and transmits light in the local `-Z` direction.

sampleZh: RectLight µר» usdLux Σ╕¡τתהτƒ⌐σ╜óΘ¥óσויµ║נ schema∩╝לτפ¿µ¥ÑµןןΦ┐░Σ╗מτƒ⌐σ╜óΣ╕אΣ╛ºσןסσויτתה intrinsic lightπאגσ«רµצ╣σ«תΣ╣יΘחלµ£אΘחםΦªבτתהτ⌐║Θק┤τ║ªσ«תµ£יΣ╕יΣ╕¬∩╝תτƒ⌐σ╜óΣ╕¡σ┐דΣ╜םΣ║מσ▒אΘד¿ XY plane∩╝לΘ╗רΦ«ñσ░║σ»╕σ£¿ X σעל Y µצ╣σנסσנהΣ╕║ 1 unit∩╝לσןסσויµצ╣σנסµ▓┐σ▒אΘד¿ -Z axisπאגΣ╣ƒσ░▒µר»Φ»┤∩╝לRectLight τתהσן»ΦºבσחáΣ╜ץσ░║σ»╕τפ▒σ▒אΘד¿ X/Y µצ╣σנסΣ╕ךτתהσ«╜Θ½רσז│σ«ת∩╝לΦאלτוºσ░הµ£¥σנסΣ╕םµר»τפ▒µƒנΣ╕¬Θó¥σñצτתהµצ╣σנסσ▒₧µאºσז│σ«ת∩╝לΦאלµר»τפ▒ prim τתה transform µךךσ▒אΘד¿ -Z µקכσט░σ£║µש»Σ╕¡τתהτ¢«µáחµצ╣σנסπאגΘרוΦ»╗µטצΦ░דΦ»ץΦ┐שΣ╕¬Θí╡Θ¥óµק╢∩╝לσוטτí«Φ«ñσ▒אΘד¿σ¥נµáח∩╝לσזםτ£כ `xformOp:rotateXYZ`πאב`xformOp:translate` σעל `xformOpOrder`∩╝לΘאתσ╕╕µ»פσוטΦ░ד `intensity` µ¢┤µ£יµץטπאג

RectLight τתהσ╕╕Φºבτפ¿Θאפµר»µ¿íµכƒµסהσ╜▒Σ╕¡τתה soft boxesπאבlinear lightsπאבfluorescent lights σעל light panelsπאגσ«דσעלτג╣σויµ║נµטצτנדσ╜óτב»Σ╕םσנל∩╝תRectLight τתהΣ║«Θד¿µ¥ÑΦח¬µ£יΘ¥óτº»τתהτƒ⌐σ╜ó∩╝לµיאΣ╗ÑΘר┤σ╜▒Φ╛╣τ╝רπאבσןםσ░הΘ½רσויσעלτוºµרמΦªזτ¢צΦלדσ¢┤Σ╝תΣ╜ףτמ░σח║Θ¥óσויµ║נσ░║σ»╕πאגσ«דΣ╣ƒΣ╕םσנלΣ║מ `PortalLight`∩╝ת`PortalLight` Θאתσ╕╕µ£םσךíΣ║מ dome light Θחחµá╖σוÑσןú∩╝לΘחםτג╣µר»σ╕«σך⌐µ╕▓µƒףσש¿σ£¿Θק¿τ¬קτ¡יτƒ⌐σ╜óσ╝אσןúσñהΘחחµá╖τמ»σóדσוי∩╝¢RectLight µ£¼Φ║½σ░▒µר»σןסσויΣ╜ף∩╝לτפ¿ `inputs:width`πאב`inputs:height`πאב`inputs:texture:file`πאב`inputs:color` σעל `inputs:intensity` τ¢┤µמÑµןןΦ┐░τƒ⌐σ╜óσןסσויΘ¥óτתהσ░║σ»╕πאבΘó£Φי▓Φ┤┤σ¢╛σעלσ╝║σ║ªπאג

σ«רµצ╣τñ║Σ╛כµךךΣ╕אΣ╕¬ `RectLight` µפ╛σ£¿ `Sphere` σעל `Cube` ΘשהΦ┐ס∩╝לΦ«╛τ╜« `inputs:width = 5`πאב`inputs:height = 5`∩╝לσ╣╢τפ¿ `checkerboard.png` Σ╜£Σ╕║ `inputs:texture:file`πאגΦ┐שΣ╕¬τñ║Σ╛כσנלµק╢σ▒ץτñ║Σ║זΣ╕יσ▒גΣ┐íµב»∩╝תτ¼¼Σ╕אσ▒גµר»σןסσויΘ¥óτº»∩╝ל5x5 τתהτƒ⌐σ╜óµ»פΘ╗רΦ«ñ 1x1 µ¢┤σדןσñºΘ¥óτº»τב»µ¥┐∩╝¢τ¼¼Σ║לσ▒גµר»Φí¿Θ¥óΘó£Φי▓∩╝לtexture color map Σ╝תΦ«⌐σןסσויΘó£Φי▓σ£¿τƒ⌐σ╜óΘ¥óΣ╕ךσןרσלצ∩╝¢τ¼¼Σ╕יσ▒גµר»τ⌐║Θק┤σןרµםó∩╝ל`xformOp:rotateXYZ = (0, 90, 0)` σעל `xformOp:translate = (3.5, 0, -1)` µךךσ▒אΘד¿ -Z σןסσויµצ╣σנסσעלσ£║µש»µסזΣ╜םΦבפτ│╗Φ╡╖µ¥ÑπאגΦכÑµ╕▓µƒףτ╗ףµ₧£τ£כΦ╡╖µ¥Ñσדןµ▓íµ£יτוºσט░τ¢«µáח∩╝לΣ╝רσוטµúאµƒÑ transform σעל `xformOpOrder`∩╝לσזםµúאµƒÑ renderer µר»σנªµפ»µלבτ¢╕σ║פτתה texture/light shaping τ╗הσנטπאג
sampleEn: RectLight is an intrinsic light that emits from one side of a rectangle. The rectangle is centered in the XY plane, emits along the -Z axis, and is 1 unit long in X and Y by default.
Use RectLights to illuminate objects, simulating soft boxes used in photography, linear lights, fluorescent lights, and light panels.

sampleZh: µ£¼Θí╡µר» OpenUSD release τפ¿µט╖µלחσםקΣ╕¡ usdLux `ShadowAPI` τתהσ«לµץ┤σןלΦ»¡σןגΦאדΘí╡πאגµ£¼Φ╜«σן¬µשכτ║º `ShadowAPI.html` Φ┐שΣ╕אΘí╡∩╝לσ╣╢Σ╗Ñσ«רµצ╣Θí╡σעלµ£¼σ£░ `source/full_release/user_guides/schemas/usdLux/ShadowAPI_source.html` Σ╕║µ║נΘí╡σ»╣τוº∩╝¢Σ╕¡µצחΦºúΘחךΦªזτ¢צσ«רµצ╣µªגΦ┐░πאב`Properties` sectionπאב5 Σ╕¬ `inputs:shadow:*` σ▒₧µאºπאבΣ╕מ `LightAPI` shadow-linking τתהΦ╛╣τץלπאבrenderer µפ»µלבσ╖«σ╝גσעלΦ░דΦ»ץΦ╖»σ╛הπאג`ShadowAPI`πאב`inputs:shadow:color`πאב`inputs:shadow:distance`πאב`inputs:shadow:enable`πאב`inputs:shadow:falloff`πאב`inputs:shadow:falloffGamma`πאב`color3f`πאב`float`πאב`bool`πאב`Shadows`πאב`LightAPI`πאב`ShapingAPI`πאב`RectLight` τ¡יΦכ▒µצח schema/API/token/σ▒₧µאºσנםΣ┐¥µלבσמƒµá╖πאג

`ShadowAPI` τתהµá╕σ┐דΦבלΦ┤úµר»τ╗שτב»σויσó₧σךáΘר┤σ╜▒σñצΦºגµמºσט╢πאגσ«רµצ╣Θí╡µךךσ«דµןןΦ┐░Σ╕║τפ¿µ¥Ñµמºσט╢ shadow behavior τתה API∩╝לΣ╛כσªג shadow colorπאבdistance σעל falloff∩╝לσ╣╢µרמτí«µןנΘזעΦ┐שΣ║¢µמºσט╢µר» `non-physical controls`πאגΦ┐שσןÑΦ»¥µר»ΘרוΦ»╗µ£¼Θí╡τתהΦ╛╣τץל∩╝ת`ShadowAPI` Σ╕םµר»µצ░τתהτ£ƒσ«₧σויΣ╝áΦ╛ףµ¿íσ₧כ∩╝לΣ╕םµי┐Φ»║Φד╜Θחןσ«טµבע∩╝לΣ╣ƒΣ╕םµ¢┐Σ╗úµ╕▓µƒףσש¿τתהτי⌐τנזΘר┤σ╜▒τ«קµ│ץ∩╝¢σ«דµןנΣ╛¢τתהµר»τ╗שσזוσ«╣Σ╜£Φאוσעל lighting artist Φ░דµץ┤Θר┤σ╜▒µר╛τñ║µצ╣σ╝ןτתה schema σוÑσןúπאג

µיאΦ░ף `non-physical controls`∩╝לµהןµא¥µר»Φ┐שΣ║¢σ▒₧µאºσן»Σ╗Ñµ£יµהןτ╗ץΦ┐חΣ╕Ñµá╝τי⌐τנזΦºהσ╛כ∩╝לΣ╗Ñµ╗íΦ╢│Θץ£σñ┤πאבτ╛מµ£»Θúמµá╝πאבσן»Φ»╗µאºµטצσנטµטנΘ£אµ▒גπאגµ»פσªגΘר┤σ╜▒σ£¿τ£ƒσ«₧Σ╕צτץלΘחלΘאתσ╕╕Σ╕םΣ╝תΦó½Σ╗╗µהןµƒףµטנµלחσ«תΘó£Φי▓∩╝לΣ╜ז `inputs:shadow:color` σובΦ«╕Σ╜£ΦאוΦ░דΦךגτב»σויµךץσ░הΘר┤σ╜▒τתהΘó£Φי▓∩╝¢τ£ƒσ«₧Σ╕צτץלΘחלΘב«µלíτי⌐σעלµמÑµפ╢Θ¥óΣ╣כΘק┤τתהΦ╖¥τª╗Σ╕םΣ╝תτפ¿Σ╕אΣ╕¬τ«אσםץΘרטσא╝σז│σ«תΘר┤σ╜▒µר»σנªσ¡רσ£¿∩╝לΣ╜ז `inputs:shadow:distance`πאב`inputs:shadow:falloff` σעל `inputs:shadow:falloffGamma` σן»Σ╗Ñτ╗שµ╕▓µƒףτ╗ףµ₧£σó₧σךáΦ╖¥τª╗Θשנσט╢σעלµ╕נΘתנµ¢▓τ║┐πאג
sampleEn: This local bilingual page explains the usdLux `ShadowAPI` user-guide page in Chinese first while retaining the official identifiers, property names, USD types, tokens, section labels, and links for comparison.
`ShadowAPI` controls shadow behavior such as color, distance, and falloff, and the official page frames these controls as non-physical artistic controls.

sampleEn: `ShapingAPI` controls the shape of a light's emission, including light spread, cone controls, focus, tint, and IES profile sampling.
Read `ShapingAPI` as directional emission shaping, not as the base light type, linking model, or shadow-control schema.

sampleEn: `SphereLight` is an intrinsic light that emits outward from a sphere and can optionally be treated as a zero-radius point light.
Use it for point-like or spherical light sources such as bulbs and headlamps; it remains a positioned scene prim.
